notes: With-skill pass rate: 88.9% vs without-skill: 38.9% — a +50 percentage point improvement
The skill adds ~4.4 seconds and ~5,875 tokens per run — minimal overhead for a 2.3x accuracy improvement

Without-skill Docker eval had the worst performance (16.7%) — Claude recommends deprecated service tokens, wrong CLI install method (npm instead of apt), and 'infisical export' instead of 'infisical run'
Without-skill Python SDK eval scored 33.3% — wrong package name ('infisical' instead of 'infisicalsdk'), wrong imports, wrong class names
Without-skill K8s eval was closest (66.7%) but still recommended deprecated Service Tokens as the primary auth method and API Keys as secondary
The one assertion the with-skill Python SDK eval missed was a false negative in the grading script (import detection edge case) — qualitatively the response was correct
Every without-skill response recommends at least one deprecated auth pattern; with-skill responses consistently use machine identities
